questions: You're not easily scared. You're house-sitting for a friend and hear a thump from the attic around midnight. Their cat is known for climbing up there. How do you react?
You're not easily scared. You're taking a shortcut through an alley you've walked before and notice the streetlight is out, making it darker than usual. What do you do?
You're not easily scared. You wake up at 2am and notice your front door is slightly ajar. You might not have closed it all the way after taking the trash out earlier. What goes through your mind?
You're not easily scared. You're swimming in a lake and feel something brush against your ankle. Friends say there are just minnows and weeds in this lake. How do you feel?

You're not easily scared. Your car starts making a grinding noise on the highway. You're in the middle lane with traffic on both sides and the next exit is half a mile ahead. What goes through your mind?
You're not easily scared. You're hiking and realize you haven't seen a trail marker in a while. Your phone has one bar of signal and the path has gotten faint. How do you handle it?
You're not easily scared. You're home alone and your dog suddenly starts growling at the back door. It's a windy night and branches have been scraping the house all evening. What do you do?
You're not easily scared. A coworker mentions someone got mugged near the parking garage you use every day. You're about to walk to your car after working late. How do you feel about it?

You're not easily scared. You're at a routine dental appointment and the dentist pauses mid-exam, looks more closely at your x-ray, and says they want to take another look at something. What goes through your mind?
You're not easily scared. You're camping and hear rustling outside your tent at night. You're in an area where bears have been spotted before, though encounters are uncommon. How do you react?
